plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to reduce extreme risk.
Sell QQQ to free up capital and reduce concentration.
Increase allocation to QQQI for income generation and stability.
Add to SPY to build a larger, stable broad index position.
Maintain a significant position in CRWD as a quality tech holding.
This conserves capital by cutting leverage and focusing on stability.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, focusing on stable income and broad market exposure. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal.

plan: Trim leveraged ETF SOXL to a minimal position.
Sell SMH to reduce semiconductor exposure.
Reduce QQQ allocation to manage concentration.
Increase QQQI to boost income generation.
Add new positions in AMD and NVDA, strong scanner setups.
Increase SPY to maintain a core broad index holding.
balanced.read: This plan targets ~2.5% per month with a risk-first approach to growth. It balances broad market exposure with quality tech and income, while reducing overall leverage. Drawdowns are managed by trimming riskier assets and adding diversification.

plan: Increase SOXL to leverage semiconductor upside.
Add SPAL conditionally, if market conditions favor.
Reduce QQQ and QQQI to free capital for high-conviction adds.
Concentrate heavily in AMD and NVDA, top scanner setups.
Maintain a position in CRWD as a strong tech performer.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into leveraged ETFs and high-conviction tech names. It accepts higher potential drawdowns and the risks of leverage decay for amplified gains. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to remove volatility.
Exit high-beta single names like CRWD and volatile sector SMH.
Reduce QQQ to free capital for income generation.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation for maximum income focus.
Add to SPY for a stable, lower-volatility broad market component.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% per month by prioritizing yield and stability. It cuts all leveraged and high-beta assets, focusing on income-generating ETFs like QQQI and a stable broad index like SPY. Drawdowns are minimized, but growth potential is also limited.
